summary: Backend-only testing for ETIeduerp iteration: Lost leads, restore-from-lost, demos-today, AI insights, cert request edit/delete (cert manager fields), exam-bookings incentive-paid by id/booking_id, and default eti_* WhatsApp templates. Wrote pytest suite (24 tests) covering all 9 features-or-bugs-to-test. ALL 24 TESTS PASSED.

minor.issue: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget exceeds quickly (Max budget: 0.001 USD seen in logs). Endpoint correctly falls back to rule-based insights with ai_powered=false. Structure (insights/recommendations/summary) is always returned with HTTP 200, which is acceptable per the review request. Consider raising the LLM budget if consistent ai_powered=true is desired.

verified_features: GET /api/leads/lost works for Admin/Branch Admin/Counsellor; 403 for FDE & Cert Manager (verified)
PUT /api/leads/{id}/restore-from-lost?new_status=Contacted moves Lost->Contacted; 400 when not in Lost; 403 for FDE
GET /api/leads excludes Lost by default and includes them when ?status=Lost is passed
GET /api/branch-admin/demos-today returns {date,count,demos} for Admin/Branch Admin; 403 for FDE/Cert Manager; new Demo Booked lead appears
PUT /api/certificate-requests/{id} accepts student_name, program_name, program_duration; persisted across GET; 403 for Counsellor
DELETE /api/certificate-requests/{id} works for Cert Manager; 403 for Counsellor; subsequent GET returns 404
GET /api/analytics/ai-leads-insights returns 200 with insights/recommendations/summary/ai_powered for Counsellor & Branch Admin; 403 for Admin (role-restricted)
PUT /api/exam-bookings/{booking_id}/incentive-paid works using both id and legacy booking_id ($or query); returns 404 only for unknown IDs

GET /api/admin/whatsapp-settings returns pre-populated events with eti_enquiry_confirmation, eti_demo_confirmation, eti_enrollment_confirmation, eti_payment, eti_fee_reminder, eti_birthday_wishes, eti_certificate
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 11,142 lines - violates 700-line guideline; should be split into routers (auth, leads, certificates, exams, whatsapp, analytics).

Several endpoints accept Optional new_status as query param without strict validation (restore_lead_from_lost silently coerces invalid values to 'New'). Consider returning 400 for invalid statuses instead of silently defaulting.
EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget appears very small (~$0.001) which causes AI insights to fall back to rule-based after a few calls. Acceptable per spec but worth flagging to product owner.

GET /api/certificate-requests/{id} returns the full document including possibly sensitive fields (verification_id, certificate_id) without role restriction beyond authenticated user - verify intended.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@etieducom.com / admin@123. Test users (auto-created, prefixed TEST_, password Test@123): test_ba_{run}@test.com (Branch Admin), test_couns_{run}@test.com (Counsellor), test_cm_{run}@test.com (Certificate Manager), test_fde_{run}@test.com (FDE).
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Branch_{run}, test users (Branch Admin/Counsellor/Cert Manager/FDE), TEST_LostLead, TEST_DemoToday leads, test certificate_requests and exam_bookings docs (with both id and booking_id legacy variants). Cleanup fixture removes TEST_-prefixed leads/cert requests and EBK- exam bookings at end of session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 9 backend features fully verified via /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_lost_leads_etc.py (24 tests, 100% pass). The test file is self-contained and creates required role users via /api/admin/users on the first run; subsequent runs reuse existing TEST_ users. AI insights endpoint may return ai_powered=false if EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget exhausted - both outcomes are valid per spec. Re-run with: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_lost_leads_etc.py -v`
